auth.title: Anvil access
auth.description: Enter the bearer token configured on the server to continue.
auth.loginHelp: Username and password for a user created in Admin → Users. On a fresh install log in as 'admin' with the first 16 characters of ANVIL_BEARER_TOKEN.

devices.title: Devices
devices.noDevices: No block devices discovered yet. Click Rescan to enumerate.
devices.history: Run history (best IOPS and BW per completed run)
devices.noHistory: No completed runs yet for this device.

runs.title: Runs
runs.abortConfirm: Abort this run? Any running fio process will receive SIGTERM. The run will be marked aborted.
runs.histogramEmpty: Select a completed phase to see its latency distribution.
runs.histogramUnavailable: No json+ histogram bins available for this phase (fio may not have emitted them).
runs.smartDiffHelp: Raw numeric SMART fields from nvme smart-log before and after the run. Deltas shown for counters; temperature shown in °C.
runs.sniaHelp: Per SNIA SSS-PTS v2.0.2 §7.2: steady state requires (max − min) ≤ 20% of mean AND linear-fit drift across the window ≤ 10% of mean, both evaluated on the 4 KiB 100%-write IOPS metric over the last 5 rounds.

newRun.title: Start a new benchmark
newRun.destructiveWarning: This profile is marked destructive. Type the last 6 characters of the device serial to proceed.
newRun.noTestable: No testable devices available. Rescan first.

models.title: Device models
models.subtitle: All storage devices that have ever been tested, grouped by brand and model.
models.noModels: No models have been tested yet.
models.noRuns: This model has no completed runs yet.
models.phaseSamplesHelp: Each bar is one run of this test on a device of this model.

system.title: System environment
system.subtitle: Host CPU / PCIe / NVMe / block-layer / load checks that influence benchmark reproducibility. Probed via the privileged runner with nsenter -t 1 -m.
system.noneInCategory: No checks match the current filter.

tune.title: One-click auto-tune
tune.help: Admin-only. Flips CPU governor to 'performance', PCIe ASPM to 'performance', NVMe I/O scheduler to 'none', queue depth to 2048, and read-ahead to 128 KiB — every tunable is transactional: if any single write fails the runner reverts every earlier successful write automatically. The receipt below lets you explicitly revert later.
tune.applyConfirm: This will write to host sysfs (CPU governor, PCIe ASPM, block scheduler, nr_requests, read-ahead) on behalf of the admin user. Any in-flight benchmark may see its environment change. Proceed?
tune.revertConfirm: Revert every change from the last apply?

compare.title: Cross-model comparison
compare.subtitle: Pick two or more device models that share at least one benchmark phase, then overlay their results for a specific metric.
compare.noCommonPhases: No benchmark phase is common to every selected model.
compare.noRuns: This model has no completed runs yet.

pcie.title: PCIe link
pcie.help: Compares what the device is capable of (LnkCap) against what the link is actually running at (LnkSta). A degraded flag means the test is NOT using the device's full PCIe bandwidth — e.g. a Gen5 x4 drive plugged into a Gen4 slot will show degraded with status=Gen4 x4.

dashboard.approxBw: sum of (write BW × runtime) across complete runs
dashboard.pcieDegradedHelp: These drives are running at a PCIe speed or width below their capability. Benchmarks on them will under-report the drive's real peak performance.
dashboard.alarmsHelp: Runs that ended in failure or abort. Thermal-abort reasons include a temperature threshold + consecutive-samples message in error_message.

admin.usersSubtitle: Create and manage Anvil users. Viewers can only read; Operators can execute runs; Administrators can also manage users.
admin.deleteConfirm: Delete user "{{username}}"? This cannot be undone.
